title: Minimize access to webhook configuration objects

input_row.title: Minimize access to webhook configuration objects
input_row.description: Users with rights to create/modify/delete validatingwebhookconfigurations or mutatingwebhookconfigurations can control webhooks that can read any object admitted to the cluster, and in the case of mutating webhooks, also mutate admitted objects. This could allow for privilege escalation or disruption of the operation of the cluster.
input_row.rationale: The ability to manage webhook configuration should be limited
input_row.audit: Review the users who have access to validatingwebhookconfigurations or mutatingwebhookconfigurations objects in the Kubernetes API.
input_row.remediation: Where possible, remove access to the validatingwebhookconfigurations or mutatingwebhookconfigurations objects References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/rbac-good-practices/#control- admission-webhooks

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_rbac_webhook_configuration_access_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all ClusterRole and Role objects in the cluster using the Kubernetes API or kubectl:
  - `kubectl get clusterrole -o yaml`
  - `kubectl get role --all-namespaces -o yaml`
- For each ClusterRole and Role, inspect the rules for verbs (get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete) on the following resources:
  - `validatingwebhookconfigurations` (group: admissionregistration.k8s.io)
  - `mutatingwebhookconfigurations` (group: admissionregistration.k8s.io)
- Identify all ClusterRoleBindings and RoleBindings that grant access to these roles:
  - `kubectl get clusterrolebinding -o yaml`
  - `kubectl get rolebinding --all-namespaces -o yaml`
- Map which users, groups, or service accounts are bound to roles with permissions to create, update, or delete the above webhook configuration resources.
- Pass Criteria: Only a minimal, controlled set of trusted users/groups/service accounts (ideally cluster administrators) have create, update, or delete permissions on `validatingwebhookconfigurations` and `mutatingwebhookconfigurations`. No unnecessary or broad access (e.g., wildcards, system:authenticated, or non-admin users) is present.
- Fail Criteria: Any non-administrative users, groups, or service accounts have create, update, or delete access to these webhook configuration resources, or if wildcards are used in a way that grants broad access.
- The program should output a report listing all subjects with such access and flag any that are not explicitly approved.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.9.0 PDF
